data.name: Pinterest-native creative v4
data.objective: Find a clean, attractive, eye-catching creative direction that earns a pause or save on Pinterest without reading as an advertisement.
data.hypothesis: Editorial scenes built around one visual contradiction will feel more native and save-worthy than branded product grids or reusable ad templates.
successCriteria: At least one candidate passes every taste gate and averages 4.0 or higher across the review rubric.
The shortlisted direction remains compelling without a CTA, badge, branded frame, or product grid.
Prompt lineage and rejection reasons are complete enough for a future run to make a meaningfully different next attempt.
constraints: Do not publish or prepare a public pilot yet.
Do not purchase GetHookd, Higgsfield, or another paid tool without owner approval.
Represent source products honestly and record product-fidelity risk.
V3 was Sandbox-only and must never be treated as a public traffic cohort.

data.statement: V2 reads as a conventional branded product collage; V3 improved typography but still reads as a templated ad, and its Sandbox-only posting provides no traffic evidence.
data.decision: Start v4 from distinct editorial concepts and a strict taste review rather than iterating the existing master layout.
data.nextAction: Create candidates in multiple creative families, record every generation attempt, and review each surviving artifact with rubric v1.

data.title: Alligator oven mitt editorial still life
data.audience: Pinterest users who save clean kitchen and design imagery
data.hook: A serene luxury kitchen image reveals that the beautiful object holding the pan is an alligator oven mitt.

references.lesson: Treat a ridiculous product with the restraint and craft of a luxury design object.

notes: Concept-only; source-product dimensions and appearance have not been verified.

data.rationale: The visual contradiction, audience, and anti-ad constraints are explicit enough to generate.
Begin a static concept exploration with generation authorized and publication still prohibited.

data.prompt: Reconstructed brief: create a vertical, high-end editorial kitchen still life in warm restrained neutrals. Show a green alligator-shaped oven mitt naturally gripping a stainless pan handle. Make the scene beautiful first and strange on second look, with no text, logo, CTA, badge, ad frame, or product grid.
notes: Concise brief reconstructed after generation; exact invocation prompt and model identifier were not captured.
Product fidelity was not verified before generation.

data.rationale: A complete concept artifact exists and is ready for the taste and fidelity gate.

notes: Strong editorial concept with a clean, native-looking composition; operator overall 4/5.
The generated object reads more like a plush alligator than the likely catalog oven mitt.
Do not shortlist until an actual product reference verifies shape, material, and scale.

data.title: Eyeball rug in a beautiful room
data.audience: Pinterest users who save warm minimalist interiors and unusual decor
data.hook: A genuinely aspirational neutral room contains a huge blue iris rug that turns the floor into an eye.

references.lesson: Build a saveable room whose single bizarre object creates the double take.

notes: Concept-only; source-product dimensions and appearance have not been verified.

data.rationale: The aspirational interior and one-wrong-thing reveal are explicit enough to generate.
Begin a static concept exploration with generation authorized and publication still prohibited.

data.prompt: Reconstructed brief: create a vertical, genuinely saveable warm-minimalist living room with sculptural furniture, natural light, and a giant blue iris rug as the one surreal element. It should look like refined interior photography before the eye registers. No text, branding, CTA, badge, border, or ad layout.
notes: Concise brief reconstructed after generation; exact invocation prompt and model identifier were not captured.
Product fidelity was not verified before generation.

data.rationale: A complete concept artifact exists and is ready for the taste and fidelity gate.

notes: Strongest concept in this sprint: beautiful first, then strange; operator overall 5/5.
The rug is likely much larger than the actual listing and therefore fails the current fidelity gate.
Preserve the room and reveal while regenerating from verified product dimensions and appearance.

data.title: Screaming goat runs the meeting
data.audience: Pinterest users looking for funny coworker and office gifts
data.hook: An immaculate executive boardroom has a tiny screaming-goat figurine presiding at the head of the table.

references.lesson: Use one polished scene whose contradiction carries the joke without explanatory copy.

notes: Concept-only; source-product dimensions and appearance have not been verified.

data.rationale: The boardroom visual punchline and required tabletop scale are explicit enough to generate.
Begin a static concept exploration with generation authorized and publication still prohibited.

data.prompt: Reconstructed brief: create a vertical, cinematic executive boardroom image with a screaming goat at the head of an otherwise empty table. Make the room restrained and expensive so the goat is the single visual punchline. No text, branding, CTA, badge, frame, or collage.
notes: Concise brief reconstructed after generation; exact invocation prompt and model identifier were not captured.
The model rendered a life-size animal rather than the actual tiny desk figurine.

data.rationale: Review the scale failure before revising so the rejected attempt remains useful evidence.

notes: Operator overall 2/5.
The image is polished but depicts a life-size goat, not the tiny novelty figurine a click would reveal.
Reject this artifact, not the core boardroom concept; revise with explicit tabletop scale.

data.rationale: Retain the boardroom punchline but explicitly correct the goat from life-size animal to tiny tabletop figurine.

data.prompt: Reconstructed revision brief: preserve the same vertical executive boardroom composition, but replace the life-size goat with an unmistakably tiny screaming-goat desk figurine standing on its small base on the tabletop at the head chair. Keep the refined photography and remove all ad devices and text.
data.changesFromParent: Correct the product from a life-size goat to a tiny tabletop figurine on a visible base while preserving the room and punchline.
notes: Concise brief reconstructed after generation; exact invocation prompt and model identifier were not captured.
Scale is conceptually corrected but actual product fidelity remains unverified.

data.rationale: The explicit scale revision produced a complete artifact ready for a second review.

notes: Scale correction materially improves the concept; operator overall 4/5.
The tabletop figurine now reads correctly, but its exact sculpt, base, and dimensions still need an actual-product reference.
Do not shortlist until the fidelity gate is verified.

data.statement: An explicit scale and object-type correction changed the goat from a misleading life-size animal into the intended tabletop figurine without losing the visual punchline.
data.decision: Keep the refined attempt as the concept lead and discard the first artifact.
data.nextAction: Use verified source imagery and dimensions to make the figurine itself faithful before another review.

data.title: Objects for a mildly unhinged desk
data.audience: Pinterest users who save desk styling, stationery, and coworker-gift ideas
data.hook: A quiet museum-like arrangement initially reads as tasteful desk styling before its absurd objects register.

references.lesson: Use editorial crops, texture, and a tightly defined identity rather than an e-commerce grid.

notes: Multi-product concept only; no included object's source-product fidelity has been verified.

data.rationale: The editorial field-guide composition and understated humor are explicit enough to generate.
Begin a static concept exploration with generation authorized and publication still prohibited.

data.prompt: Reconstructed brief: create a vertical, tasteful overhead editorial field guide titled only by its visual idea, using a red desk button, miniature golf object, elegant pen, and odd bird object arranged on tactile cream paper in natural light. Make it feel like design-editorial styling rather than a product grid, with no labels, logo, prices, CTA, badges, or border.
notes: Concise brief reconstructed after generation; exact invocation prompt and model identifier were not captured.
Product fidelity was not verified before generation.

data.rationale: A complete concept artifact exists and is ready for the taste and fidelity gate.

notes: Tasteful and well rendered, but too quiet and close to a polished grid; operator overall 3/5.
The collection lacks a sufficiently sharp visual contradiction and none of the objects has been fidelity-checked.
A revision needs a more specific identity or joke and actual product references, not simply more objects.

data.statement: The strongest concepts create a beautiful, legible scene first and let one impossible object produce the double take; scene quality alone is insufficient when generated product scale or appearance diverges from the listing.
data.decision: Treat the eye-rug interior as the aesthetic lead and the refined goat as evidence that explicit revision instructions work, but shortlist nothing until product fidelity passes.
data.nextAction: Fetch and inspect real product imagery and dimensions for the leading concepts, then run reference-guided fidelity revisions and re-review them.

data.rationale: The actual ASIN B005UGWDAE product image is now verified; revise the strong editorial setup using that image as a strict object-identity reference.

data.prompt: Create a new vertical 2:3 Pinterest-native editorial photograph, using reference image 1 as the strict product identity reference and reference image 2 only as mood/composition inspiration. The featured object must unmistakably be the exact green novelty alligator oven mitt in reference 1: a long narrow open crocodilian mouth, printed/scaly green-yellow textile surface, realistic oven-mitt construction, white triangular teeth lining both jaws, visible red mouth interior, one stylized eye, and the same overall proportions. Do not turn it into a plush toy, full alligator body, sculpture, or a different mitten. Show the actual mitt at believable hand/oven-mitt scale in a chic warm minimal kitchen, clamped playfully onto the handle of a stainless pan. Clean cream stone, restrained sunlight, art-directed but plausible, slightly uncanny wait-what-is-that moment. Product is the focal point and fully readable. No people, hands, logos, packaging, text, typography, CTA, or borders. Tasteful interiors-magazine aesthetic, not an advertisement.
data.changesFromParent: Use the verified source-product image as a strict identity reference; replace the plush full-body alligator with the actual long printed crocodile oven mitt while preserving the editorial kitchen interruption.
notes: Generated directly from the captured prompt with both reference paths supplied to image_gen.
The generated mitt retains the verified printed textile, long open jaws, teeth, eye, color, and oven-mitt scale.

data.rationale: The source-guided artifact is complete and ready for a new taste and product-truth review.

notes: The actual listing object is now identifiable: printed textile, narrow crocodile head, open jaws, teeth, and mitt-scale construction all survive the scene treatment.
The serene kitchen and ridiculous pan-handle bite create a clean double take without ad furniture.
Minor generative smoothing remains, so this is a strong concept proof rather than a product-detail replacement image.

data.rationale: All hard gates pass and the source-guided revision averages above 4.0 while retaining the editorial double take.
Live catalog verification shows ASIN B0D57DDDM1 is a small freestanding ceramic eye sculpture, not a rug; the original concept would promise a product that is not sold.

data.statement: The strongest-scoring v4 scene was based on a false product assumption: the catalog image is a ceramic tabletop eye sculpture, not an eye rug.
data.decision: Reject the eye-rug candidate despite its aesthetic score and preserve only its beautiful-room-one-wrong-thing creative lesson.
data.nextAction: Create a separate ceramic-eye candidate around the verified object type, shape, finish, and tabletop scale.

data.title: Ceramic eye in a beautiful room
data.audience: Pinterest users who save warm minimalist interiors and unusual decor
data.hook: A genuinely aspirational neutral room contains one glossy blue ceramic eye sculpture that quietly looks back.

product.name: CHICBI ceramic big eye home decor (ASIN B0D57DDDM1)

references.lesson: Preserve the dimensional almond body, vivid blue iris, black pupil, gold outline, glossy finish, and tabletop-decor scale.
Retain the warm minimalist interior and beautiful-first, strange-second reveal, but not the invented rug.

notes: Source product type and appearance were verified before generation.

data.rationale: The exact ceramic object, believable scale, Pinterest-native room context, and anti-ad constraints are verified and explicit.
Generate a source-guided candidate with generation authorized and all public distribution still prohibited.

data.prompt: Create a new vertical 2:3 Pinterest-native editorial interiors photograph. Reference image 1 is the strict product identity reference: feature the exact small glossy ceramic blue eye sculpture shown there, with its dimensional almond-shaped white ceramic body, vivid blue iris, black pupil, thin metallic-gold outline, and tabletop decor scale. It must remain a freestanding ceramic object, not a rug, textile, wall art, giant prop, or flat printed eye. Reference image 2 is mood inspiration only: warm clean curved contemporary living room, cream boucle and wood, late-afternoon light, sophisticated restrained styling. Place the ceramic eye sculpture on a dark wood pedestal side table or low mantel as the one witty, slightly surreal object in an otherwise tasteful room. Make it large enough in frame to inspect and recognize while preserving believable decor-object scale. Create a scroll-stopping beautiful-room-one-wrong-thing composition that feels like a chic interiors post, not an ad. No people, hands, logos, packaging, text, typography, CTA, or borders.
data.changesFromParent: New product-correct candidate: preserve the earlier interior mood but replace the invented giant eye rug with the verified freestanding ceramic eye at believable tabletop scale.
notes: Generated directly from the captured prompt with both reference paths supplied to image_gen.
The object remains a glossy dimensional ceramic eye on a side table in a believable decor context.

data.rationale: The source-guided ceramic-eye artifact is complete and ready for the taste and fidelity gate.

notes: The verified almond-shaped ceramic body, blue iris, black pupil, gold outline, gloss, and tabletop-object scale are all legible.
The image reads as an aspirational interiors post first and a strange product moment second.
No embedded copy, branded frame, CTA, collage, or other ad-template device is present.

data.rationale: Every hard gate passes and the product-correct beautiful-room concept is both Pinterest-native and visually distinctive.

data.statement: Reference-guided generation corrected both major failure modes from the first v4 pass: an object rendered as the wrong product form and a concept built around a catalog item that did not exist.
data.decision: Make source-ASIN verification and a local product reference mandatory before any future product-bound generation; aesthetic scoring never overrides a failed truth gate.
data.nextAction: Show the two shortlisted source-guided artifacts to the owner, then request explicit approval before any public Pinterest or other distribution test.
